source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A body A (m = 1 kg) is attached to the top of a vertically fixed spring of spring constant 1000 N/m and then the mass is released at the initial position P with the spring being initially in its natural length \ell_0. When A comes back to original position P another body B of equal mass falls on A with a velocity v = 0.2*\sqrt{60} m/s at t = t_0 and the collision is perfectly inelastic. Maximum amplitude of oscillations of the combined mass is?

However, the question text already contains the main physical quantities and event sequence, so image reliance is not scored as extreme
multi_step: Solving requires combining spring-motion setup, return-to-position timing/state, an inelastic collision, and the resulting oscillation amplitude
The problem involves at least two distinct reasoning phases: pre-collision motion of body A and post-collision motion of the combined mass
The answer is a derived numeric quantity rather than a direct lookup from the text
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a specific numeric answer ('0.04 m')
There are 4 multiple-choice options, giving an additional discrete check target
The physics process is formula-based and can be independently recomputed from the provided quantities
